original_image_tags: The document is a single, aged, off-white page of typed text. The paper shows significant bleed-through, with the typed text appearing mirrored, indicating this is the reverse side of a document where the ink from the front has bled through. The page has two hole-punch marks near the top edge. There are several stamps and handwritten notations at the bottom of the page.

elements.description: Back of a document page with faint, mostly illegible typed text visible through the paper. At the bottom, there are three distinct stamped or typed annotations in blue, red, and black ink: 'MAR 24 7 11 AM '69', 'MAR 21 69 1', 'REC'D - CORR & IDS', and 'INCONS APR 101969' along with handwritten text 'Matters, 4/28/69 REK'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree the page shows bleed-through typed text and stamps/annotations at the bottom, but B provides specific quoted stamp text while A does not, and neither contradicts the other on the visual content.
